verdict_reasoning: RAC1 is a small GTPase from the Rho family, a soluble cytoplasmic signaling protein. It carries a C-terminal prenylation motif (farnesylation/geranylgeranylation) that anchors it to the inner leaflet of the plasma membrane and intracellular membranes, but not the outer face. RAC1 lacks a transmembrane domain, GPI anchor, or any mechanism to reach the extracellular surface. It functions as an intracellular regulator of cytoskeletal dynamics and is not a target of surface-directed therapeutics.
